stories.headline: Rafael Grossi: the next Iran nuclear deal will look very different
stories.summary: IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi says the 2015 Iran nuclear deal is no longer a workable model.

stories.summary: Treasuries hovered near a three-week high ahead of this week’s first batch of jobs numbers that could cement the case for the Federal Reserve to deliver an interest-rate hike under new Chair Kevin Warsh.

stories.summary: The fund has drawn backlash from critics who said it was a scheme to reward President Trump’s political allies with public benefits.

stories.headline: Why India’s Run as a Stock Market Darling May Be Over
stories.summary:
India's stock market is losing its allure.

The South Asian nation stands out as one of the biggest losers as factors like the AI trade reshape global investment flows. Asmi Bhatia with more. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Marvell Surges After Huang Calls It the Next $1 Trillion Company
stories.summary: Marvell Technology Inc. shares surged 26% in premarket trading on Tuesday after Nvidia Corp.’s Jensen Huang predicted that the semiconductor and networking company will be the next business to hit a $1 trillion valuation, more than five times its current capitalization.

stories.headline: Segantii’s Sadler Details Rising Stress at Time of Esprit Trades
stories.summary: Segantii Capital Management founder Simon Sadler told a Hong Kong court that stresses had been building at home and in his professional life by 2017, the year his firm is accused of trading on inside information.

stories.headline: Insight Investment Is Lured by 6% Yields Into Battered UK Bonds
stories.summary: Insight Investment is the latest asset manager to take a punt on long term UK bonds after this year’s selloff, betting that the highest yields in the developed world will be enough to make up for the risks.

stories.headline: UBS Offers First AT1 Since Swiss Authorities Paused Reforms
stories.summary: UBS Group AG is set to raise Additional Tier 1 capital in US dollars, its first foray into the market since Switzerland paused plans to tighten rules on the riskiest type of bank debt.

stories.summary: Extended positioning in US technology stocks is putting investors at risk of a reversal, according to Citigroup Inc. strategists.

stories.headline: PE-Owned JetBio Plans Landmark Green Jet Fuel Plant in Brazil
stories.summary: Private equity-controlled JetBio plans to build the world’s biggest commercial green jet fuel plant that uses ethanol in Brazil to take advantage of the country’s ample feedstock alternatives.

stories.headline: Korean Firms Turn to Short-Term Funding as Bond Yields Climb
stories.summary: South Korean firms have increasingly turned to short-term financing as rising government bond yields drive up borrowing costs in the corporate debt market.

stories.headline: Galaxy Digital Sets Up Prediction Market OTC Derivatives Desk
stories.summary: Crypto finance conglomerate Galaxy Digital Inc. has launched a trading desk to offer large investors better access to prediction markets via over-the-counter derivatives similar to those used in markets from interest rates to commodities.

stories.headline: Philip Morris Cuts Outlook on $500 Million Canada Writedown
stories.summary: Philip Morris International Inc. cut its profit forecast for this fiscal year after writing down the value of its investment in its Canadian affiliate by $500 million.
